manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to describe a target word for another player to guess.

Rules:
(a) You have to reply in the form: CLUE: <some text>. Guesses from the other player will start with GUESS.
(b) You cannot use the target word itself, parts or morphological variants of it in your description.
(c) In addition, the same rules apply for related words which are provided below.

End conditions:
(i) If you use the target word or a related word in your description, then you lose.
(ii) If the other player can guess the target word in 3 tries, you both win.

Let us start.

This is the target word that you need to describe and that the other player needs to guess:

toilet

Related words are:

- flush
- restroom
- urinate

Important: You are under time pressure, give short descriptions that are to the point!

message.content: CLUE: A porcelain bowl with a seat, typically found in a bathroom, used for disposing of human waste.
message.reasoning:
We need to give a clue without using target or related words. Target: toilet. Related: flush, restroom, urinate. Can't use any of these words or morphological variants. So can't say "toilet", "toilets", "toileting", "flush", "flushed", "restroom", "restrooms", "urinate", "urinating", "urine", "pee"? Not sure if "pee" considered morphological? Probably related to urinate. Safer avoid "pee". Clue should hint.

We must say "CLUE: ..." Provide short description. Avoid banned. Could say "CLUE: A porcelain fixture found in bathrooms for personal hygiene needs." But "bathroom" may be okay; "restroom" not allowed. So "bathroom" okay. "Porcelain fixture" okay. Mention "white bowl with seat". Avoid flush? Could say "water-sprinkled cleaning mechanism" maybe too close. But "flushing" is banned. So cannot mention "flush". Could say "water disposal". That's not a banned word. So "CLUE: A porcelain bowl with a lid, often found in a bathroom, used for disposing of waste." That seems fine. Avoid "restroom". Use "bathroom". Avoid "urinate". Use "excrete". That is okay. Provide short. Let's produce.

You can make one guess at each trial. You win when you guess the target word. You lose when you cannot guess it in 3 tries.

Make your guesses by just saying the word using the following form: GUESS: <a word>

Let us start.


CLUE: A porcelain bowl with a seat, typically found in a bathroom, used for disposing of human waste.
